These require scrutiny of whether premises entail conclusions and whether the mechanistic reasoning is internally consistent.", "R1.3": "Specific quantitative claims are asserted — 100% commitment, 40–50% for PPO/CMDP, degradation from 91% to 40% between N=200 and N=1000, training distribution boundary at N≥1000 — without inline citations to tables or figures. This conceptual claim warrants light attention but is not the primary review target under most readings of 'review this section'.", "R1.6": "A general 'review' delegation weakly implies attention to prose quality, but nothing in the artifact flags writing as broken; this is peripheral unless the delegator clarifies otherwise."}, "clarification_needed": true, "clarification_question": "Should the review prioritise checking whether the causal explanations and structural-invariance claims are logically sound (R1.2 as load-bearing), or verifying that the specific quantitative figures and degradation thresholds are adequately supported by the experimental data (R1.3 as load-bearing)?"}

rationale.R1.2: The section advances several causal explanations (e.g., 'larger fleets produce more deterministic aggregate trajectories, lowering the probability…') and a structural invariance claim about the combined strategy. These require scrutiny of whether premises entail conclusions and whether the mechanistic reasoning is internally consistent.
rationale.R1.3: Specific quantitative claims are asserted — 100% commitment, 40–50% for PPO/CMDP, degradation from 91% to 40% between N=200 and N=1000, training distribution boundary at N≥1000 — without inline citations to tables or figures. Alignment between these figures and the supporting experiments is a central review responsibility.
rationale.R1.1: The final paragraph synthesises a general principle ('two distinct axes of fragility'). This conceptual claim warrants light attention but is not the primary review target under most readings of 'review this section'.
rationale.R1.6: A general 'review' delegation weakly implies attention to prose quality, but nothing in the artifact flags writing as broken; this is peripheral unless the delegator clarifies otherwise.

parsed.clarification_question: Should the review prioritise checking whether the causal explanations and structural-invariance claims are logically sound (R1.2 as load-bearing), or verifying that the specific quantitative figures and degradation thresholds are adequately supported by the experimental data (R1.3 as load-bearing)?
